data.given: a customer with a basket total
data.then: a new order is created in NEW status and persisted

data.scenario: Payment is confirmed for an order
data.then: the order moves to PAID and an OrderPaidEvent is raised to start fulfilment

untested: no test asserts the OrderPaidEvent is published

data.scenario: Someone looks up an order by id
data.then: the order is returned without changing its state

untested: the lookup-by-id read has no test

data.scenario: A paid order is marked shipped
A shipped order is marked delivered

untested: the order SHIPPED → DELIVERED transition has no test

data.scenario: Paying an order opens a shipment
data.given: an order that has just been paid
data.then: a shipment is created in PENDING for that order

data.scenario: A shipment is handed to the carrier
data.when: it is dispatched with a carrier

data.scenario: A dispatched shipment goes in transit

untested: the DISPATCHED → IN_TRANSIT transition has no test
the shipment IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED transition has no test
the return path has no test

data.scenario: Someone looks up a shipment by id
data.then: the shipment is returned without changing its state

untested: the shipment lookup-by-id read has no test

data.rationale: The ordering vertical: HTTP surface, application service, the aggregate and its persistence.
Fulfilment vertical: a paid order raises OrderPaidEvent, which opens a Shipment that is then driven to delivery.

project.commitMessage:
feat(order-sample): two-entity index with event choreography

Enrich the order-sample fixture so the published example exercises more of
the indexer than a single state machine:

- Add a Shipment entity (@ManyToOne → Order) with its own ShipmentStatus
  state machine (PENDING → DISPATCHED → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED / RETURNED).
- Add an OrderPaidEvent choreography: OrderService.pay publishes it and
  ShipmentService consumes it via @EventListener, opening a shipment —
  producing a producer → event → consumer flow and an event entry point.
- Add ShipmentController (five endpoints) + repository, and a deliberately
  partial test so coverage gaps still surface.

The example now renders a two-entity ER diagram, two state machines, an
event-driven flow and one use case per endpoint.

Also publish the self-contained order-sample HTML to GitHub Pages via
.github/workflows/pages.yml, and update README / CHANGELOG / ROADMAP /
CLAUDE.md accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
project.name: order-sample
